medical_history.chief_complaint: Amniotic band at the fetus’s ankle and toes involving a left thick edematous foot and a right edematous club foot as well as an umbilical cord without abnormal blood flow
medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Presented at 18 weeks’ gestation with amniotic band at the fetus’s ankle and toes. All toes were visible on both feet. Distal arterial and venous blood flow was present within the edematous feet. Movement of the ankles, including flexion, could not be visualized during an extensive ultrasound examination. The position of the feet in utero remained unchanged during 3 consecutive ultrasound examinations over 2 weeks.

physical_examination.specialty_examination: Left foot appeared pale and edematous in its distal portion. The right foot presented with 2 necrotic toes. Multiple amniotic sheets were observed, twisted and wrapped around the left ankle, the umbilical cord, and the right clubfoot.

radiographic_examinations.findings: Amniotic band at the fetus’s ankle and toes involving a left thick edematous foot and a right edematous club foot. All toes were visible on both feet. Distal arterial and venous blood flow was present within the edematous feet. Movement of the ankles, including flexion, could not be visualized.

The limb edema appeared to have been reduced, distal blood flow was documented through both feet, and active movements could be observed from both ankles and feet, although the right foot remained permanently in equinovarus position.
Resolution of the edema and normal movements of both feet.

other_tests.findings: Multiple amniotic sheets were observed, twisted and wrapped around the left ankle, the umbilical cord, and the right clubfoot. The left foot appeared pale and edematous in its distal portion. The right foot presented with 2 necrotic toes (not suspected prior to surgery). Lysis of the amniotic sheets was performed on both feet using laser dissection.
other_tests.changes_over_time: Immediate in utero recoloration of the left foot was observed following the band section.

diagnosis.final_diagnosis: Amniotic band syndrome with limb constriction
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The diagnosis was based on the presence of amniotic bands causing bilateral limb constrictions, confirmed via detailed fetal ultrasound examination. The indication for surgery was major limb lymphedema with associated umbilical cord involvement. The reasoning included the observation of limb edema downstream from the amniotic band, suggesting vascular impact, and the potential for in utero amputation if left untreated.
